reasoning:
The base rate of Russia invading a NATO country is historically zero. Since the beginning of the Ukraine war, Russia's conventional military capacity is severely constrained as they are heavily engaged in Ukraine. Intelligence and expert consensus (e.g., German BND, Baltic intelligence, NATO observations) estimate that Russia would need 3 to 10 years after the conclusion of hostilities in Ukraine to reconstitute a force capable of a large-scale war against NATO. 

While there are ongoing escalations such as 'hybrid/gray zone' sabotage campaigns and minor border/airspace incidents (e.g., the removal of Narva River buoys in May 2024, or a Russian jet violating NATO airspace in May 2025), there is zero evidence of a planned or imminent military offensive intended to 'establish control over any portion of any NATO country.' 

Furthermore, there are only four months left in 2025 (as the forecast date is August 31, 2025). The logistical footprint required for an invasion of NATO territory would be visible months in advance, and there are currently no intelligence warnings of such an imminent troop build-up on NATO borders. The probability is set to the absolute minimum allowed by the platform (2%), reflecting the near-impossibility of this event occurring before December 31, 2025.
